cards.title: 'Parcel held by customs'
cards.script: Calls / WhatsApps saying a parcel in your name is held by customs and you must pay a fee to release it. Real customs sends a written notice, never collects fees by phone.
cards.example: Your courier is held by customs, pay ₹8000 to release or face arrest.

cards.title: 'Digital arrest' / CBI / ED call
cards.script: Caller claims to be CBI / ED / Mumbai Police, says a parcel in your name has narcotics or your Aadhaar is linked to a crime. Holds you on a video call and demands money. There is no such thing as a 'digital arrest' in Indian law.
cards.example: FedEx parcel in your name has narcotics. CBI officer here. You are under digital arrest.

cards.title: Fake 'FIR' demanding instant payment
cards.script: Tells you an FIR is registered against you and demands an immediate transfer to 'settle' it. Real police never demand payment over phone.
cards.example: I am from CBI, there is an FIR against you. Pay ₹50,000 immediately.

red_flag_rules.red_flag: 'Your parcel is held by customs, pay X to release' is a known scam. Indian customs never calls or WhatsApps you to demand fees. If a parcel is held, they send a written notice to your address.

'Digital arrest', 'narcotics found in your parcel', 'CBI/ED case' calls are 100% scams. There is no such thing as a 'digital arrest' in Indian law. Real police never demand money or hold you on a video call.
They are threatening arrest unless you pay. This is a fear-based scam. Hang up. Do not transfer money. Do not stay on the call.

next_steps: Hang up the call. Do not stay on a video call with anyone claiming to be police, CBI, ED or customs.
Do NOT transfer any money. Customs / police in India never collect fees on phone or WhatsApp.
Call 1930 and file a complaint at https://cybercrime.gov.in.
If they have a copy of your Aadhaar / PAN, file a complaint with the bank and freeze account access. Block the number.
